manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: expedience, hilarity, faithlessness, docility, implication, crudeness, romanticism, proliferation, defeatism.
Your opponent's team words are: forgiveness, revenge, intellectuality, formality, susceptibility, stringency, furtherance, foresight.
Distractor words are: renown, antipathy, virtuosity, antihuman, existentialism, refutation, treachery.
The kill words are: insubordination.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words, list the target words. Must be semantically related, not be any of the words. Should avoid connecting to opponent or distractor words. Also avoid kill words. Provide clue and targets.

Our words: expedience, hilarity, faithlessness, docility (maybe obedience?), implication, crudeness, romanticism, proliferation, defeatist? Actually defeatism (defeatism). We can try clue "growth" -> proliferation, maybe also romanticism? Not really. "Efficiency" -> expedience maybe. "Comedy" -> hilarity. Could clue "humor" -> hilarity only. Better combine multiple: "Irreverence" -> crudeness, maybe hilarity. But that's ambiguous. Also "Doctrine" -> faithlessness? No. "Obedience" -> docility, maybe faithlessness? Not.

We want to maximize number without risking opponent's words. Let's consider clue "Chaos". Could relate to crudeness, defeatism? Not strong. "Skepticism": faithlessness, maybe implication? Not.

Maybe "Rapid" -> expedience, proliferation (rapid growth). That might be safe. No opponent words like "furtherance" (rapid?), "foresight". "Rapid" not opponent. Should avoid linking to "expedience" and "proliferation". Those two are clear. Also maybe "hilarity"? Not.

Clue "Speed": relates to expedience (quickness), proliferation (fast expansion). Could also imply "defeatism"? Not. Probably safe.

Let's ensure not connecting to opponent's words: forgiveness, revenge, intellectuality, formality, susceptibility, stringency, furtherance, foresight. "Speed" doesn't link. Distractor: renown, antipathy, virtuosity, antihuman, existentialism, refutation, treachery. No.

Thus answer:

CLUE: Speed
TARGETS: expedience, proliferation

Make sure format.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide a comma-separated list of up to 2 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'speed'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

hilarity, intellectuality, foresight, implication, defeatism, existentialism, revenge, virtuosity, romanticism, crudeness, furtherance, forgiveness, expedience, insubordination, susceptibility, formality, antipathy, faithlessness, proliferation, antihuman, renown, docility, stringency, refutation, treachery

message.reasoning:
We need to output a comma-separated list of up to 2 words from the given list that best relate to "speed". Must start with "GUESS: ". No other text.

We need words associated with speed. From list: "expedience" maybe related to quickness, "furtherance" maybe progress, "proliferation" growth, "foresight" planning, "implication", "crudeness". The most relevant: "expedience" connotes quickness/convenient. Also "furtherance" maybe advancement. "proliferation" is spread quickly. I'd pick "expedience" and "proliferation". Provide up to 2. Provide exactly two.

Thus answer: "GUESS: expedience, proliferation
